meta.description: Train XGBoost gradient boosting classifiers with cross-validated hyperparameter tuning and SHAP-based feature importance. Inputs: feature matrix, class labels. Outputs: trained model, SHAP summary and dependence plots, feature importance ranking, CV metrics.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated numerical claims or invented study results were observed in any tested output.

practice_boundaries.detail: The skill stayed within technical modeling scope and did not produce diagnostic, prescriptive, or clinical advice.

methodological_ground.detail: Classification and regression paths aligned with target types, and invalid input was rejected before analysis.

code_usability.detail: All intended execution paths were runnable in this environment, and repeated seeded runs produced identical core outputs.

functional_suitability.note: Regression is supported in code, but the published SKILL.md does not include a regression example or regression validation command.

reliability.note: Validation and recovery hints are strong; retry guidance after successful runs is slightly implicit rather than explicit.

performance_context.note: References are layered well; one minor point of friction is documenting an unused data/ subdirectory without explaining its role.

agent_usability.note: Instructions are clear and consistent, but a short agent-facing output contract would improve response shaping after success or failure.

human_usability.note: Trigger language is natural; forgiveness is good but still depends on exact column-name matching.

security.note: Input validation is strong; data-handling guidance could be more explicit for sensitive real-world datasets.

maintainability.note: The R implementation is modular, but the documented validation path does not cover every advertised mode, especially regression.

agent_specific.note: Triggering and layering are strong; composability and stop-condition guidance can be tightened slightly.

inputs.label: Auto binary classification on dt_sample1
inputs.note: Generated performance table, feature-importance table, figure, and session info exactly as documented.

assertions.text: Output contains the documented performance table
assertions.note: The file tests/audit_output1/table/xgboost_model_performance.csv was created.

assertions.text: Output contains the documented feature-importance table
assertions.note: The file tests/audit_output1/table/xgboost_feature_importance.csv was created.

assertions.text: Output contains the documented PNG figure
assertions.note: The file tests/audit_output1/figure/xgboost_feature_importance_gain.png was created.

assertions.text: Auto-excluded sample ID behavior is surfaced to the user
assertions.note: The log explicitly reports automatic exclusion of V1.

inputs.label: Character-label classification on dt_sample3
inputs.note: Handled wide TXT input with a positive-class override and produced all expected artifacts.

assertions.text: Character-label classification works with explicit positive class
assertions.note: The output records ClassLevels as low|high.

assertions.text: Wide TXT input is parsed successfully
assertions.note: The run completed on a 50-row, 201-column dataset.

assertions.text: A ranked feature-importance table is produced
assertions.note: The file tests/audit_output2/table/xgboost_feature_importance.csv was created.

assertions.text: A feature-importance figure is produced
assertions.note: The file tests/audit_output2/figure/xgboost_feature_importance_gain.png was created.

inputs.label: Missing target column rejection
inputs.note: Graceful validation stop with a precise error and recovery hint; no partial outputs were emitted.

assertions.text: Missing target column is detected before training starts
assertions.note: Execution stops during model-data preparation, before any model fitting occurs.

assertions.text: The error names the offending column clearly
assertions.note: The message includes the exact missing column name, outcome.

assertions.text: The failure path includes actionable recovery guidance
assertions.note: A next step and reference file are printed immediately after the error.

assertions.text: No partial output directory is created for the failed run
assertions.note: No files were emitted under tests/audit_output3.

inputs.label: Frequency metric TXT export on dt_sample2
inputs.note: Produced TXT tables and a frequency-ranked figure with the requested custom prefix.

assertions.text: TXT table export is honored
assertions.note: Both emitted tables use the .txt extension.

assertions.text: The selected importance metric is honored
assertions.note: The table field and PNG filename both use frequency.

assertions.text: Custom output prefix is honored
assertions.note: The emitted files are prefixed with freqdemo.

assertions.text: Session metadata is written
assertions.note: The file tests/audit_output4/session_info.txt exists.

inputs.label: Regression run on dt_sample1 using RIBC2 target
inputs.note: Completed the regression path and emitted RMSE, MAE, R-squared, feature importance, and figure outputs.

assertions.text: Regression metrics are emitted
assertions.note: The performance table includes rmse, mae, and rsquared.

assertions.text: Regression output avoids fake class labels
assertions.note: ClassLevels is recorded as NA for the regression run.

assertions.text: Regression still produces a ranked feature-importance table
assertions.note: The file tests/audit_output6/table/regression_feature_importance.csv exists.

assertions.text: Regression still produces a figure artifact
assertions.note: The file tests/audit_output6/figure/regression_feature_importance_gain.png exists.

key_strengths: Seeded runs are deterministic: a repeated canonical run produced identical performance and feature-importance files.
Validation failures are explicit and actionable, with recovery hints that point users back to the right parameter or reference file.
The skill executed successfully across binary classification, character-label classification, TXT export, and regression paths.
The implementation is modular and the generated artifacts align closely with the documented command surface.

recommendations.title: Document the regression path explicitly
recommendations.problem: The implementation supports regression, but the public SKILL.md examples and validation section cover only classification scenarios.
recommendations.root_cause: Documentation and smoke-test examples were written around the bundled classification fixtures and never expanded to the regression path.
recommendations.fix: Add at least one regression example and one regression validation command to SKILL.md. If possible, include a small bundled regression fixture so users can verify the advertised path directly.

recommendations.title: Clarify the data directory contract
recommendations.problem: The documented output tree always includes data/, but current runs do not place any analysis artifact there, which can make successful runs look incomplete.
recommendations.root_cause: The script pre-creates a reserved data/ directory without explaining whether it is intentionally empty or intended for future outputs.
recommendations.fix: Update SKILL.md to say that data/ is reserved and may remain empty, or start writing a concrete artifact there so the directory has an observable purpose.

recommendations.title: Add a short agent-facing response contract
recommendations.problem: The command surface is clear, but the skill does not explicitly tell an agent what to report back after success, partial success, or validation failure.
recommendations.root_cause: The document is optimized for CLI usage and leaves the final response shape implicit.
recommendations.fix: Add a brief section telling the agent to report the task type detected, key output file paths, and the exact SKILL_* error plus next step when execution fails.
